title: My Favourite Season
summary: A man describes why autumn is his favourite season, talking about the colours of the leaves, the cooler weather, and his favourite autumn activities.
body: Of all the seasons, autumn is my favourite. I know many people prefer summer because of the warm weather and long days, but I love the feeling that autumn brings. The temperature starts to drop in March and the days become shorter, but I find that refreshing after the heat of summer. The thing I notice most is the colour of the trees. The leaves change from green to bright red, orange, and yellow, and on a sunny afternoon the whole forest seems to glow. I love going for long walks in the bush to enjoy the colours and breathe in the cool, clean air. There is also something special about autumn food. The farmers' markets are full of pumpkins, apples, and pears. I enjoy making soups and warm pies at the weekend, and the smell of something baking in the oven always makes the house feel cosy. Autumn is also the season when the football season kicks off, so my weekends are busy with watching and playing matches. The evenings grow dark early, which means more time inside with a book or a film. I know winter is just around the corner, but for now I am enjoying every cool, colourful day of autumn.

annotations.exampleEn: The temperature starts to drop in late October.

annotations.meaningVi: thấy điều đó thật dễ chịu / sảng khoái
annotations.exampleEn: After a long day at work, a walk outside is refreshing.

annotations.phrase: the whole forest seems to glow
annotations.meaningVi: cả khu rừng dường như tỏa sáng
annotations.exampleEn: In the evening light, the city seems to glow.

annotations.phrase: breathe in the cool, clean air
annotations.meaningVi: hít thở không khí trong lành, mát mẻ
annotations.exampleEn: I opened the window to breathe in the fresh air.

annotations.meaningVi: chợ nông sản / chợ của người nông dân
annotations.exampleEn: We buy fresh vegetables at the farmers' market every Saturday.
The living room felt cosy with the fire on.

annotations.meaningVi: bắt đầu (thường dùng cho mùa giải thể thao hoặc sự kiện)
annotations.exampleEn: The new football season kicks off next weekend.

annotations.meaningVi: buổi tối trời tối sớm hơn
annotations.exampleEn: In winter, evenings grow dark very early.

annotations.phrase: winter is just around the corner
annotations.meaningVi: mùa đông gần kề / sắp đến rồi
annotations.exampleEn: Pack warm clothes — winter is just around the corner.

annotations.meaningVi: vào một buổi chiều nắng đẹp
annotations.exampleEn: On a sunny afternoon, the park is full of people.

annotations.meaningVi: những chuyến đi bộ dài trong rừng / bụi cây hoang dã (Úc)
annotations.exampleEn: We went for long walks in the bush during our camping trip.

grammarNotes.title: Comparatives with adjectives: 'the days become shorter', 'cooler weather'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng tính từ so sánh hơn để so sánh hai điều. Với tính từ ngắn (một âm tiết), thêm '-er': short → shorter, cool → cooler, long → longer. Với tính từ dài (hai âm tiết trở lên), dùng 'more': beautiful → more beautiful, refreshing → more refreshing. Bài học này dùng nhiều so sánh hơn: 'shorter', 'cooler', 'darker'. Sau so sánh hơn, dùng 'than': 'cooler than summer'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use comparative adjectives to compare two things. Short adjectives (one syllable) add '-er': short → shorter, cool → cooler, dark → darker. Long adjectives use 'more': refreshing → more refreshing. This passage uses several comparatives: 'shorter days', 'cooler weather', 'grow dark early.' After comparatives, use 'than': 'cooler than summer.' Note: some adjectives are irregular — good → better, bad → worse.

grammarNotes.title: 'I love + verb-ing' and 'I enjoy + verb-ing' for expressing preferences
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Sau 'love', 'enjoy', 'like', 'hate', 'prefer' ta dùng danh động từ (verb + -ing), không phải động từ nguyên thể. 'I love going for long walks.' 'I enjoy making soups.' 'I enjoy baking.' Đây là cấu trúc rất phổ biến để nói về sở thích và thói quen. So sánh: 'I love to go' (cũng đúng với 'love/like' nhưng không dùng được với 'enjoy'). 'I enjoy to bake' — SAI. 'I enjoy baking' — ĐÚNG.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: After verbs of liking and preference — love, enjoy, like, hate, prefer — use the gerund (verb + -ing), not the infinitive. Examples: 'I love going for walks.' / 'I enjoy making soups.' Note: 'enjoy' ALWAYS takes a gerund, never a 'to' infinitive. 'I enjoy to cook' is wrong; 'I enjoy cooking' is correct. 'Love' and 'like' can take either the gerund or the infinitive, but the gerund sounds more natural when talking about habits.

translationVi: Trong tất cả các mùa, mùa thu là mùa yêu thích của tôi. Tôi biết nhiều người thích mùa hè vì thời tiết ấm áp và ngày dài, nhưng tôi yêu cảm giác mà mùa thu mang lại. Nhiệt độ bắt đầu giảm vào tháng Ba và những ngày trở nên ngắn hơn, nhưng tôi thấy điều đó thật sảng khoái sau cái nóng của mùa hè. Điều tôi chú ý nhất là màu sắc của cây cối. Lá cây chuyển từ xanh sang đỏ tươi, cam và vàng, và vào một buổi chiều nắng đẹp, cả khu rừng dường như tỏa sáng. Tôi thích đi bộ dài trong rừng để thưởng thức màu sắc và hít thở không khí trong lành, mát mẻ. Cũng có điều gì đó đặc biệt về thức ăn mùa thu. Các chợ nông sản đầy ắp bí ngô, táo và lê. Tôi thích làm súp và bánh nướng ấm vào cuối tuần, và mùi thức ăn đang nướng trong lò luôn khiến ngôi nhà cảm thấy ấm cúng. Mùa thu cũng là mùa bóng đá bắt đầu, nên những cuối tuần của tôi bận rộn với việc xem và thi đấu. Buổi tối trời tối sớm hơn, nghĩa là có nhiều thời gian hơn trong nhà với một cuốn sách hoặc một bộ phim. Tôi biết mùa đông sắp đến rồi, nhưng hiện tại tôi đang tận hưởng từng ngày mát mẻ, đầy màu sắc của mùa thu.

questions.prompt: What is the narrator's favourite season?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'Of all the seasons, autumn is my favourite.'
questions.hint: He states it clearly in the first sentence.

questions.prompt: Why do many people prefer summer, according to the narrator?
options: For the rain and green trees
For the warm weather and long days
questions.explanation: 'I know many people prefer summer because of the warm weather and long days.'
questions.hint: He gives two reasons when talking about summer.

questions.prompt: What does the narrator notice most about autumn?
questions.explanation: 'The thing I notice most is the colour of the trees.'
questions.hint: He describes the leaves in detail.

questions.prompt: What colours do the leaves turn in autumn?
questions.explanation: 'The leaves change from green to bright red, orange, and yellow.'

questions.prompt: What activity does the narrator love doing in autumn?
options: Going for long walks in the bush
questions.explanation: 'I love going for long walks in the bush to enjoy the colours.'
questions.hint: He mentions both the activity and the reason.

questions.prompt: What can you find at the farmers' markets in autumn?
questions.explanation: 'The farmers' markets are full of pumpkins, apples, and pears.'
questions.hint: Three types of autumn produce are named.

questions.prompt: What does the narrator enjoy cooking at the weekend?
questions.explanation: 'I enjoy making soups and warm pies at the weekend.'
questions.hint: He mentions two types of warm food.

questions.prompt: What does the smell of baking make the house feel?
questions.explanation: 'The smell of something baking in the oven always makes the house feel cosy.'
questions.hint: It is a warm, comfortable feeling.

questions.prompt: What sport does the narrator mention in the passage?
questions.explanation: 'Autumn is also the season when the football season kicks off.'
questions.hint: It is the most popular team sport mentioned.

questions.prompt: What does the narrator do in the evenings in autumn?
options: Spend time inside with a book or film
questions.explanation: 'The evenings grow dark early, which means more time inside with a book or a film.'
questions.hint: The dark evenings lead to indoor activities.

questions.prompt: What season is coming after autumn, according to the narrator?
questions.explanation: 'I know winter is just around the corner.'
questions.hint: He mentions it is coming soon.

questions.prompt: When does the temperature start to drop, according to the narrator?
questions.explanation: 'The temperature starts to drop in March.'

blanks.explanation: 'autumn' — the narrator says it is his favourite season.
'drop' — the temperature starts to drop (decrease) in March.
'colour' — the narrator notices the colour of the trees most.
'glow' — the passage says 'the whole forest seems to glow.'
'pumpkins' — the passage says 'full of pumpkins, apples, and pears.'
'pies' — the narrator enjoys making 'soups and warm pies.'
'cosy' — baking makes the house feel cosy (warm and comfortable).
'kicks' — 'kicks off' is a phrasal verb meaning 'begins', especially for sports seasons.
'dark' — 'the evenings grow dark early' in autumn.
'corner' — 'just around the corner' is an idiom meaning 'coming very soon.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator describes autumn as his favourite season and lists many reasons why he enjoys it. Do you have a favourite season? What activities, foods, or feelings make a particular season special for you, and how does the weather in your country affect your daily life?
